evidence_text: COMPASS is a hierarchical framework with three specialized components: a MainAgent, a Meta-Thinker, and a ContextManager.
source_excerpt: "We propose COMPASS (Context-Organized Multi-Agent Planning and Strategy System), a lightweight hierarchical framework that separates tactical execution, strategic oversight, and context organization into three specialized components: (1) a MainAgent ... (2) a Meta-Thinker ... and (3) a ContextManager ..."
note: The paper presents this as the core architectural decomposition.
caveat: The paper presents this as the core architectural decomposition.

evidence_text: The paper identifies context management as the central bottleneck in long-horizon agent reasoning.
source_excerpt: "We identify context management as the central bottleneck—extended histories cause agents to overlook critical evidence or become distracted by irrelevant information, thus failing to replan or reflect from previous mistakes."
note: This is the authors' framing of the main failure source for long-horizon tasks.
caveat: This is the authors' framing of the main failure source for long-horizon tasks.

evidence_text: Across GAIA, BrowseComp, and Humanity's Last Exam, the paper reports accuracy gains of up to 20% relative to single-agent and multi-agent baselines.
source_excerpt: "Across three challenging benchmarks—GAIA, BrowseComp, and Humanity's Last Exam—COMPASS improves accuracy by up to 20% relative to both single- and multi-agent baselines."
note: This is a headline aggregate performance claim rather than a single table row.
caveat: This is a headline aggregate performance claim rather than a single table row.

evidence_text: With Gemini 2.5 Pro on BrowseComp, COMPASS scores 35.4 Pass@1.
source_excerpt: In Table 2 under "Gemini 2.5 Pro," the COMPASS row reports BrowseComp = "35.4".
note: This is the reported Pass@1 value for that model-benchmark pairing.
caveat: This is the reported Pass@1 value for that model-benchmark pairing.

evidence_text: With Gemini 2.5 Pro on GAIA and HLE, COMPASS scores 67.8 and 31.7 Pass@1, respectively.
source_excerpt: In Table 2 under "Gemini 2.5 Pro," the COMPASS row reports GAIA = "67.8" and HLE = "31.7".
note: These are the reported GAIA and HLE Pass@1 values for base COMPASS with Gemini 2.5 Pro.
caveat: These are the reported GAIA and HLE Pass@1 values for base COMPASS with Gemini 2.5 Pro.

evidence_text: Adding test-time scaling raises COMPASS to 43.7 on BrowseComp, 72.1 on GAIA, and 35.2 on HLE with Gemini 2.5 Pro.
source_excerpt: In Table 2, "COMPASS-TTS (Gemini 2.5 Pro)" reports BrowseComp = "43.7", GAIA = "72.1", and HLE = "35.2".
note: This evidence is about the scaled variant, not the base COMPASS system.
caveat: This evidence is about the scaled variant, not the base COMPASS system.

evidence_text: Removing the Meta-Thinker drops BrowseComp Pass@1 from 35.4 in the full system to 15.2.
source_excerpt: Table 3 lists the full system at "35.4" Pass@1, while the Meta-Thinking ablation "None (Main + Context only)" reports "15.2".
note: This supports the contribution of the Meta-Thinker through ablation, but it does not by itself isolate all causal pathways beyond the altered component.
caveat: This supports the contribution of the Meta-Thinker through ablation, but it does not by itself isolate all causal pathways beyond the altered component.

evidence_text: Removing the ContextManager drops BrowseComp Pass@1 from 35.4 in the full system to 26.4.
source_excerpt: Table 3 lists the full system at "35.4" Pass@1, while the ContextManager ablation "None (No ContextManager)" reports "26.4".
note: This is ablation evidence for the ContextManager's contribution; it shows performance loss under removal but does not fully specify why the loss occurs.
caveat: This is ablation evidence for the ContextManager's contribution; it shows performance loss under removal but does not fully specify why the loss occurs.

evidence_text: The paper reports that Context-12B achieves performance comparable to Gemini 2.5 Flash on BrowseComp while using about 70% of the tokens.
source_excerpt: "As shown in Figure 4, we evaluated the performance of Context-12B on Browsecomp, achieving performance comparable to a larger models (Gemini 2.5 Flash) while using only 70% of their tokens with a SFT-DPO training pipeline."
note: This is a reported efficiency comparison from the paper's specialized context-manager extension.
caveat: This is a reported efficiency comparison from the paper's specialized context-manager extension.

evidence_text: In COMPASS-TTS, accuracy improves as the number of parallel samples increases, but token cost also rises, and gains plateau around n = 4.
source_excerpt: "Figure 5 plots accuracy and total tokens for n ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8}. All methods improve monotonically over n = 1, with Full-PS showing the strongest gains but steepest token growth, MT-PS offering a balanced middle ground, and CM-PS achieving the best efficiency. Performance plateaus around n = 4, suggesting that n = 2–4 offers a practical sweet spot to balance accuracy and costs."
note: This evidence includes an explicit cost-performance tradeoff, so the efficiency caveat should remain attached downstream.
caveat: This evidence includes an explicit cost-performance tradeoff, so the efficiency caveat should remain attached downstream.

evidence_text: The evaluation is limited to controlled settings centered on searching, text-based browsing, and code execution, and the study primarily uses proprietary frontier models.
source_excerpt: "We propose COMPASS (Context-Organized Multi-Agent Planning and Strategy System), a lightweight hierarchical framework that separates tactical execution, strategic oversight, and context organization into three specialized components: (1) a MainAgent ... (2) a Meta-Thinker ... and (3) a ContextManager ..."
note: The paper presents this as the core architectural decomposition.
caveat: The paper presents this as the core architectural decomposition.
